proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher levels of the ordinal feature_057 (values 0/1/2) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher continuous feature_011 is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher continuous feature_006 is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Patients with feature_035 = 1 have a higher probability of objective_response than patients with feature_035 = 0.
Higher continuous feature_099 is associated with a higher probability of objective_response.
Higher continuous feature_092 is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher continuous feature_063 is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate declines monotonically with feature_057: 0.213 (n=17592) at level 0, 0.155 (n=24971) at level 1, 0.112 (n=7437) at level 2. Chi-square p=5.5e-98; logistic-regression slope per unit = -0.384, p=1.8e-97. Strongest single predictor in the dataset.
Mean feature_011 is 3.38 in responders vs 3.95 in non-responders (Welch t-test p=7.2e-39). Direction matches hypothesis.
Mean feature_006 is 58.2 in responders vs 60.3 in non-responders (Welch t-test p=9.5e-20). Direction matches hypothesis.
Response rate is 22.1% (n=3624) when feature_035=1 vs 16.5% (n=46376) when feature_035=0; chi-square p=3.5e-18.
Mean feature_099 is 3.83 in responders vs 3.80 in non-responders (Welch t-test p=1.3e-6). Direction matches hypothesis (positive association).
Mean feature_092 is 19.07 in responders vs 20.40 in non-responders (Welch t-test p=1.3e-5). Direction matches hypothesis.
Mean feature_063 is 5.65 in responders vs 6.17 in non-responders (Welch t-test p=4.4e-7). Direction matches hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092, feature_057 (ordinal), and feature_035 each maintain an independent association with objective_response in a multivariable logistic regression that adjusts for the others.

analyses.result_summary: All seven predictors remain significant after mutual adjustment: feature_057 (z=-20.9), feature_011 (z=-12.2), feature_006 (z=-9.1), feature_035 (z=+8.5), feature_099 (z=+4.9), feature_063 (z=-4.9), feature_092 (z=-4.4). Pseudo-R^2 = 0.019. Effects are largely additive on the log-odds scale.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092, and feature_035, patients with feature_005='black' have a lower probability of objective_response than patients with feature_005='white'.

After the same adjustment, patients on Medicaid (feature_087='medicaid') or uninsured patients (feature_087='uninsured') have a lower probability of objective_response than patients with private insurance.

analyses.result_summary: race_black coefficient = -0.109 (p=0.005); race_other = -0.196 (p=0.030); race_asian = -0.079 (p=0.13); race_hispanic = -0.036 (p=0.30). Black patients have significantly lower adjusted odds of response than white patients; the disparity is in the hypothesized direction.

analyses.code: Same model with insurance dummies (private = reference).
analyses.result_summary: ins_medicaid = +0.020 (p=0.59); ins_medicare = +0.042 (p=0.12); ins_uninsured = +0.010 (p=0.87). No insurance category is significantly associated with response after adjustment - hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the multi-level integer features feature_096, feature_064, feature_018, feature_045, feature_042, and feature_125 each show a monotonic association with objective_response (analogous to feature_057).

analyses.code: for c in multi_cols: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(df[[c]])).fit()  # treat as numeric
analyses.result_summary: Linear-trend logistic slopes: feature_096 +0.007 (p=0.51), feature_064 +0.014 (p=0.21), feature_018 +0.004 (p=0.34), feature_045 -0.020 (p=0.07), feature_042 -0.010 (p=0.35), feature_125 -0.023 (p=0.04). Only feature_125 reaches nominal significance and the magnitudes are tiny. Hypothesis is largely refuted - feature_057 is the only monotone ordinal predictor with a meaningful trend.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_035=1 on objective_response varies by feature_057 stratum (interaction): the risk-difference for feature_035 is larger in the better-PS strata.

analyses.result_summary: feature_035 main = +0.34 (p=2.3e-8), feature_057 main = -0.38 (p=1.8e-89), interaction term = +0.027 (p=0.67). Risk differences across PS strata (035=1 vs 035=0): PS=0: 26.97% vs 20.82%; PS=1: 20.48% vs 15.14%; PS=2: 15.31% vs 10.88%. Effects are additive on the logit scale - hypothesis NOT supported (no statistical interaction).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_035=1 modifies the slope of one or more continuous predictors (feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092) on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction p-values: 011 p=0.23, 006 p=0.39, 063 p=0.35, 099 p=0.024 (coef -0.094), 092 p=0.88. Only feature_099 shows a nominally-significant interaction (slightly diminished benefit of feature_035 in patients with high feature_099). Overall, biomarker effect on continuous predictors is largely absent - hypothesis weakly supported only for feature_099.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pairs of top continuous predictors interact multiplicatively in their effect on objective_response (i.e., the joint effect is non-additive on the log-odds scale).

analyses.result_summary: Of the 15 pairwise interactions among feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092, and feature_057, only feature_006 x feature_092 is nominally significant (interaction coef -0.036, p=0.0056). All others have p > 0.09. Effects of these continuous covariates are essentially additive - hypothesis largely refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the seven main-effect predictors, additional binary features (notably feature_093 and feature_014) retain residual independent associations with objective_response.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols not in adjust: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(base.assign(B=df[c]))).fit()
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment, residual associations: feature_093 coef = +0.125 (p=0.0035, prev=8%), feature_014 coef = +0.059 (p=0.017, prev=40%), feature_012 coef = -0.048 (p=0.050, prev=42%), feature_121 coef = -0.088 (p=0.050, prev=8%), feature_030 coef = +0.103 (p=0.053, prev=5%). These are real but small residual effects, and most other binaries are null. Hypothesis supported for feature_093 and feature_014.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The most prevalent binary feature feature_085 (prevalence 55%) is associated with objective_response (it could be a sex or other demographic indicator).

analyses.result_summary: Response rate: 17.0% when feature_085=0 (n=22490) vs 16.8% when feature_085=1 (n=27510). Chi-square p~0.55. Hypothesis NOT supported - feature_085 has no association with response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_035=1 on objective_response is modified by at least one binary feature in the dataset (i.e., feature_035 is a biomarker that interacts with other treatment- or comorbidity-like indicators).

analyses.code: for c in all binaries: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(pd.DataFrame({'a':df.feature_035,'b':df[c],'ab':df.feature_035*df[c]}))).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Three highly significant feature_035 x binary interactions emerge: feature_035 x feature_027 (interaction coef = +1.05, p=1.1e-32), feature_035 x feature_014 (coef = +0.73, p=7.7e-18), and feature_035 x feature_058 (coef = +0.70, p=1.0e-3). All three interaction terms are positive, indicating these binaries substantially amplify the response benefit of feature_035=1. Hypothesis strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_035, feature_027, and feature_014 jointly determine response: only patients positive for all three (the triple-positive subgroup) show a substantially higher response rate, with a significant 3-way interaction term.

analyses.result_summary: Full saturated 3-way model: all main effects and 2-way interactions are non-significant; the 3-way interaction term feature_035 x feature_027 x feature_014 = +2.14 (p=3.0e-29). Triple-positive subgroup (n=427): response rate 62.1%; all other 7 cells of the 2x2x2 table: response rates 16-17% (overall n=49573, rate=16.5%). This is the strongest signal in the dataset and points to a marker-defined combination-therapy effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_035=1 patients, the joint presence of feature_027=1 AND feature_014=1 increases response, while either alone does not (i.e., the combination matters, not the components).
Within feature_035=0 patients, neither feature_027 nor feature_014 (alone or in combination) affects response.

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_035=1 (n=3624): cell rates were 027=0/014=0 17.3% (n=1569), 027=0/014=1 16.1% (n=1008), 027=1/014=0 16.8% (n=620), 027=1/014=1 62.1% (n=427). Logistic regression: 027 main coef -0.04 (p=0.78), 014 main coef -0.09 (p=0.43), 027x014 interaction coef = +2.18 (p=4.6e-32). Strongly supports hypothesis - benefit requires both 027 and 014 simultaneously in marker-positive patients.

Within feature_035=0 (n=46376): cell rates 16.6-17.0% across all four 027x014 cells. Logistic regression: 027 main p=0.05, 014 main p=0.44, 027x014 interaction coef = +0.04 (p=0.52). Strongly supports hypothesis - in marker-negative patients neither component nor combination affects response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_058 acts as an alternate biomarker: among feature_058=1 patients, the combination feature_027=1 and feature_014=1 also produces an elevated response rate (analogous to feature_035).

analyses.code: d=df[df.feature_058==1]; sm.Logit(...).fit(); also test 035 x 058 x 014 saturated
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_058=1 (n=1467): 027x014 interaction coef = +0.55 (p=0.067), borderline. The combo+ cell rate is 22.9% vs 16% baseline - much smaller effect than seen with feature_035. In the saturated 035 x 058 x 014 model the 3-way term is non-significant. feature_058 and feature_035 are essentially independent (chi-square p>0.5; co-occurrence n=119). Hypothesis only weakly supported - feature_058 is at best a minor accessory marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_035=1 patients, the absolute response rate within the 027+014 combination subgroup decreases as feature_057 (PS-like) increases, but the multiplicative effect of the combination (on the log-odds scale) is similar across PS strata.

analyses.result_summary: Within marker+ stratum (n=3624), combo cell rates by feature_057: PS=0 combo+ 68.4% (n=152) vs combo- 21.6% (n=1179); PS=1 combo+ 64.4% (n=205) vs combo- 14.8% (n=1572); PS=2 combo+ 41.4% (n=70) vs combo- 11.2% (n=446). PS main coef = -0.42 (p<1e-7), combo main coef = +2.21 (p<1e-29), PS x combo interaction coef = -0.08 (p=0.62). Multiplicative effect of combo is constant across PS strata; only the absolute response rate shifts. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the marker+ combination-treated subgroup (feature_035=1 AND feature_027=1 AND feature_014=1), patients with feature_005='black' have a lower probability of response than white patients - i.e., the racial disparity persists even when the optimal targeted regimen is delivered.

analyses.result_summary: Triple-positive subgroup (n=427) response rates: white 64.8% (n=281), asian 63.2% (n=19), hispanic 63.0% (n=73), black 47.1% (n=51), other 33.3% (n=3). Race-adjusted logistic: race_black coef = -0.73 (p=0.018), other races non-significant. Even within the high-response, marker+combo+ subgroup, black patients have significantly lower odds of response than white patients. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A unified multivariable logistic regression incorporating top continuous predictors, feature_057, feature_035, feature_027, feature_014, the (feature_027 AND feature_014) combination, the feature_035 x combination interaction, and race dummies fits the data substantially better than a model with main effects only.

analyses.code: main = Logit(y, X_main).fit(); inter = Logit(y, X_main + ['combo','feature_035*combo']).fit(); LR test
analyses.result_summary: Main-effects-only model: AUC=0.598, log-likelihood -22292.2. Adding combo + (feature_035 x combo): AUC=0.605, LL -22100.4. Likelihood-ratio chi-square = 383.7 on 2 df, p ≈ 0 (numerical underflow). Final model coefficients confirm previous findings: feature_035 main collapses to ~0, combo main ~0, (feature_035 x combo) coef = +2.20 (p<1e-77). Hypothesis strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Apart from feature_035, no other binary marker substantially modifies the effect of the (feature_027 AND feature_014) combination on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Top binary x combo interactions: feature_035 inter coef +2.13 (p=7.2e-74) - dominant. All other interactions are weak: feature_013 -0.25 (p=0.014), feature_080 -0.27 (p=0.016), feature_036 -0.29 (p=0.023). None comes close to the magnitude or significance of feature_035. Hypothesis supported - feature_035 is the unique biomarker for this combination.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patient age (feature_078) is associated with the probability of objective_response, with younger patients responding more often.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind by response; logistic on z(age); age-quintile dummies; PS x age interaction
analyses.result_summary: Mean age in responders 68.03 vs non-responders 67.98 (p=0.72). Univariate logistic slope per SD: +0.004 (p=0.72). Adjusted slope: +0.003 (p=0.77). Quintile-stratified rates 16.2-17.8%, no monotone trend. Age x feature_057 interaction p=0.23. Hypothesis NOT supported - age has no detectable effect on response in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Insurance type (feature_087) is independently associated with objective_response after adjusting for clinical predictors and race.

analyses.code: Adjusted full model adding insurance dummies
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted insurance coefficients (private = reference): medicaid +0.020 (p=0.59), medicare +0.042 (p=0.12), uninsured +0.010 (p=0.87). Likelihood ratio chi-square ~5 on 3 df, p~0.18. Hypothesis NOT supported - insurance is not associated with response after adjusting for clinical predictors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_093 (binary, prev 8%) has a small positive independent effect on objective_response after adjustment for the dominant predictors.
feature_084 (continuous) has a small positive independent effect on objective_response after adjustment.

analyses.code: Adjusted logit adding feature_093 to base seven-predictor model
analyses.result_summary: feature_093 coef = +0.125 (p=0.0036). Patients with feature_093=1 have ~13% higher log-odds of response, independent of the dominant predictors. Direction matches hypothesis.

analyses.code: Adjusted logit adding z(feature_084) to base seven-predictor model
analyses.result_summary: feature_084 standardized coef = +0.027 (p=0.026). Each SD higher feature_084 raises log-odds of response by 0.027 - small but positive and consistent with hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the residual binary features whose unadjusted effects appeared marginal (feature_121, feature_111, feature_080, feature_038, feature_024, feature_053, feature_091), none retains a significant negative association with objective_response after adjustment for the seven dominant predictors.

analyses.code: Adjusted logit screen for each binary
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted p-values: feature_121 -0.088 (p=0.050), feature_111 -0.077 (p=0.061), feature_080 -0.051 (p=0.18), feature_038 -0.056 (p=0.22), feature_024 -0.052 (p=0.13), feature_053 -0.178 (p=0.17, prev=1%), feature_091 -0.217 (p=0.10, prev=1%). Most fail to reach significance. feature_121 hovers at p~0.05 - hypothesis largely supported (these features carry little independent information).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014 has a positive interaction with at least one binary feature OTHER than feature_035; specifically feature_118 amplifies the feature_014 effect on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Top feature_014 x binary interactions: feature_118 +0.224 (p=0.0054), feature_016 +0.125 (p=0.019), feature_115 -0.119 (p=0.025), feature_107 +0.65 (p=0.034), feature_043 +0.104 (p=0.036). The effects are modest compared with the feature_035 x feature_027 x feature_014 interaction. feature_118 nominally amplifies feature_014 - hypothesis supported, but the effect is much smaller than the dominant biomarker-combination signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The (feature_027 AND feature_014) combination has no detectable effect on response in marker-negative (feature_035=0) patients, even when stratified by other clinical features.

analyses.code: d=df[df.feature_035==0]; for stratum in [feature_058, feature_057]: response in (combo+/-) within strata
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_035=0 stratum, combo+ vs combo- response rates: overall 16.1% vs 16.6%. Stratified by feature_058: 058=0 combo+ 16.1% (n=5433) vs combo- 16.6% (n=39595); 058=1 combo+ 16.4% (n=165) vs combo- 15.0% (n=1183). No detectable benefit. Hypothesis strongly supported - the combination is genuinely inert in marker-negative patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race-based response disparity (lower response in feature_005='black' patients) is concentrated in the marker+combo+ subgroup; in marker-negative patients there is little or no race effect on response.

analyses.code: Stratified comparison: race-black response rate vs white in (a) feature_035=0, (b) feature_035=1 & combo-, (c) feature_035=1 & combo+
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_035=1 stratum: combo- subgroup white 17.6%, black 15.8%, hispanic 14.2%, asian 15.9% (small differences); combo+ subgroup white 64.8%, black 47.1%, hispanic 63.0%, asian 63.2% - black response is ~18 pp lower than white. The disparity is most clinically meaningful where the treatment works best (marker+combo+). Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A parsimonious 'best' model for objective_response includes: feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_092, feature_099 (continuous), feature_057 (ordinal PS-like), feature_035 (biomarker), feature_027 and feature_014 (treatment-component indicators) with their (feature_035 x feature_027 x feature_014) interaction, plus race dummies. This model achieves AUC ~0.605 and provides a clinically actionable summary.

analyses.code: Final logistic regression with the variables above; AUC via Mann-Whitney; pseudo-R^2
analyses.result_summary: Final model: AUC = 0.605, pseudo-R^2 = 0.027, log-likelihood = -22100.4. Coefficients: feature_011 -0.158, feature_006 -0.113, feature_063 -0.069, feature_099 +0.062, feature_092 -0.056, feature_057 -0.390, feature_035 +0.005 (NS), feature_027 -0.070, feature_014 -0.026, combo (027 AND 014) +0.042, (feature_035 x combo) +2.20. Race_black -0.110 (p=0.005). Single dominant interaction (feature_035 x 027 x 014) increases predicted response from baseline ~17% to ~62% in the triple-positive cell. Hypothesis supported.
